author: @hoofheartedicemelted296
text: A PC fan with tubercles.  What a brilliant idea.  You could surround a VAWT with a shroud, put tubercles on the edges of the shroud blades then put tubercles on the turbine blades themselves, then 3d print lots of Viktor Schauberger Whorl pipes, and attach them to the shroud blades to direct Wind Vortices, and of course the turbine blades would be Ugrinsky.  Coloured smoke should reveal the AeroDynamics.

author: @tehspamgozehere
text:
So the lumps funnel the fluid over the surface. Same flow, smaller surface area, thus less friction. Focused pressure zones. I like it.

Though I find myself wondering where the tipping point is. The optimal size of tubercle to the effected area. Whether there is the option to thin out areas that then have less pressure against them and less structural integrity required.

So many more questions!

author: @NickNightingaleYT
text: Love your channel! I'm delighted that you have an episode about this.  I have a personal experience with tubercles and have my own opinion on how it's used in nature. I made my own tubercle fins for my surfboards, and I love how they behave. I spent about 5 years experimenting with them. My impressions of tubercles are more toward them being a means to manage the effect of a foil moving through a viscous medium. If you were to stick your hand out your car window at highway speed with your fingers together and pointed towards the wind, pitching your hand up or down would have a fast and snappy response. Your arm and shoulder would feel it! If you spread your fingers apart like the tines of a comb and do the same thing, the effect is much more manageable. I believe that this is how the humpback whale benefits from its tubercles. I suspect that concept is missed on engineers focusing on the aerodynamic/hydrodynamic aspects and how they might make foils more efficient with lift and flow. Humpback tubercles are not exact or uniform, so that leads me to believe that they serve a different purpose for the whale. Tubercles or scalloped leading edges are found on sharks too. Hammerheads have bumpy leading edges. Also, some sharks have them on the leading edges of their tail fins. Cheers!

author: @cosmicseekin
text: Domestic Nuclear Battery -  OK :-)   I have no idea if this is already done on a workable scale.  My invention is:-  Based on the Principal of a Diamond Battery.  Scale the whole concept up to a Domestic Energy source.  The fuel would be spent nuclear reactor fuel which is presently put in containers in massive water ponds.  Take a reasonable piece of that decaying fuel.  Encase it in a Glass Substrate and encase that in a small energy conversion box.  The heat/energy from the Radio Active decay can be used to charge batteries to run a house.  The whole thing could be suitably shielded in underground chambers.  When the energy generation drops off after a couple of years, the Glass Capsule would be replaced.  What do you think of this ?

author: @Chris-op7yt
text:
sycamore seed shape is for maximum lift but also for gentlest flight, and for germ to land intact.

whale front fins are mostly for manouvering, with almost all thrust from tail fin..in a fluid more viscous than air.

we can get clues about efficiency from nature but, may not line up perfectly with efficiencies we want in a rotor.

author: @wrekced
text: @TNTOmnibus You need to look at aU.S. patent 11,719,220 B2 August 8, 2023. It is a wind turbine with tubercles! Looks like you're not the only one to think of this...Way to go! Your ideas were published before this patent was granted. I think that means your design counts as "prior art" so you could actually challenge the patent! Not that it would be worth the effort, but it does protect you from the patent holder taking you to court over your ideas. I love how you put these ideas out for us all to enjoy and experiment with. Thanks!

author: @mikejones-vd3fg
text: Wow thats so cool, its like organic design.   Yeah blender is steep, i started the glazed donut tutorial on youtube and sure enough you can make a convincing 3d donut in a few youtube videos, but you dont remember any of the shortcuts you used , thats  seems to be the skill, remembering where everything is to do the things you want.    Then Ii asked AI to draw me a 3d glazed donut with sprinkles and it came out 100x bettter then mine.  Im using a variation as my avatar.  You dont have the same customization if you learn to do you yourself however,  once you learn that then you can really get creative rather then just relying on AI to make something cool.   But as a non artist, ill take the AI shortcut.  Maybe there will be something for CAD soon.  just verbally type what you want done to a model and it does it.  Stil not as good as being able to carve it out smoothly yourself,  thats where we need VR Cad tools,  they find even non gamers do well in VR games because 3d is so intuative to operate in.  Id imagine it would help with CAD creation too.  Being able to work with your  hands digitially.
